title: Murraya crenulata
taxonomy: Murraya crenulata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the orange jasmine family. It is native to China and Southeast Asia. This plant belongs to the genus Murraya, which consists of about 4-5 species. Murraya crenulata is a small to medium-sized shrub or tree, known for its fragrant white flowers and small, yellow fruits. The plant is widely cultivated for its ornamental value and is often used as a hedge or topiary in tropical and subtropical regions.
uses: Murraya crenulata has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which can help in treating wounds and infections. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its antidiarrheal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Murraya crenulata is used to treat fever and colds. Additionally, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant properties.
benefits: Murraya crenulata, also known as orange Jessamine, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and flowers are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with fever and respiratory issues. The plant is also said to possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress. Some studies suggest that Murraya crenulata may have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential natural remedy for certain infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Murraya crenulata for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Murraya crenulata contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and limonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is also rich in ascorbic acid, which acts as an antioxidant and has anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, Murraya crenulata contains coumarins and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral activities. side_effects: Murraya crenulata, commonly known as orange Jessamine, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. However, there is limited scientific research on its side effects. intro: Murraya crenulata, also known as yellow jessamine or orange jessamine, is a flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to tropical Asia and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Murraya crenulata include limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
